semantic.role: Define patterns for extracting symbol declarations and usages from Kotlin source code for the ArchSpine mirror system.
responsibilities: Define export patterns for Kotlin constructs: functions (including private and suspend), classes, data classes, sealed classes, interfaces, objects, companion objects, properties (val/var), type aliases
Define usage patterns for function calls and property accesses
outOfScope: Specify project-specific policies, naming conventions, or architectural rules
Describe the algorithm for interpreting or applying these patterns
Cover other programming languages beyond Kotlin

English.purpose: This document defines a set of pattern definitions that enable the ArchSpine mirror system to parse Kotlin source code and identify all significant symbols (imports, functions, classes, variables, etc.) and their usages (calls, property accesses). It serves as a configuration for the code model extraction engine.
English.context_and_audience: This configuration file is intended for developers and maintainers of the ArchSpine project, particularly those working on the code analysis components. It is not a user-facing document but a machine-readable specification that drives the parsing logic.
key_takeaways: The document uses a pattern-based approach to describe Kotlin language constructs.
Each pattern has an id, a pattern string with placeholders like $NAME, $ARGS, $BODY, and a kind classification.
Exports are categorized into kinds (e.g., Function, Class, Interface, Variable, Type) for different symbol types.
Usages capture relationships such as function calls and property accesses between symbols.
